metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC token per Approach content area (e.g. a phrase from each of the four bullets) or add a note that the Approach section's four bullets serve as the content specification the executor must follow verbatim.
evidence: Approach enumerates four distinct contract aspects to document
AC only checks: `assert all(x in s for x in ('Code-review project criteria','round_a','criteria eval','configuration error','effective routing'))`
A single sentence 'Code-review project criteria use round_a fan-in, criteria eval, configuration error handling, and effective routing.' satisfies the AC without covering the four aspects
findings.finding: The Approach section requires four substantive content areas (Round A/B fan-in, repository-effective routing, criteria eval path, configuration-error behavior), but the AC only verifies presence of five token strings — a one-sentence stub containing all five strings satisfies the AC without documenting any of the four content areas meaningfully. There is a gap between 'what we'll do' and 'what done looks like'.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (single AC) vs. Approach section bullet list
scenarios: An executor writes a minimal stub section that passes the grep check but omits the substantive contract documentation described in Approach, leaving the actual regression-prevention goal unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the AC assertion to also check for one representative phrase from each of the four Approach bullets, e.g. add `'fan-in'` (Round A/B), `'repository-effective'` (routing), `'resolution'` or `'admission'` (criteria eval path), and `'prompt/eval spec is missing'` (config-error behavior) to the `all(x in s ...)` list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and correct the Context premise: `docs/llm-framework.md` does not currently state that projects may add code-review criteria via the overlay — update the Context to say the overlay mechanism is *undocumented* in llm-framework.md (not *partially documented*).
evidence: Full read of docs/llm-framework.md: zero hits for 'project', 'criteria', 'overlay', 'plan-review' in the body.
search_files for 'project criteria overlay code-review' across docs/llm-framework.md returned no matches.
The doc does reference 'four-pass code-review GATE' at line 344, but contains no statement about project-supplied criteria or the overlay mechanism.
findings.finding: The plan's Context section asserts that `docs/llm-framework.md` 'says projects may add code-review criteria through the same overlay as plan-review', but a full read of `docs/llm-framework.md` finds no such statement — the file contains zero mentions of 'project', 'overlay', 'criteria', or 'plan-review' in the body text. The premise that llm-framework.md already surfaces the project-criteria overlay capability for code review is false.
findings.location: Context / Problem section — 'docs/llm-framework.md documents the four-pass contract and says projects may add code-review criteria through the same overlay as plan-review'
scenarios: A reviewer relying on this premise to understand what the documentation task is filling in will misread the gap: the current doc is missing more context about the overlay mechanism than the plan implies, which could cause the author to write too thin a section.

If the motivation premise is false, the plan's framing ('the section the other five children establish is undocumented') is correct in conclusion but for slightly different reasons — the doc never stated the overlay capability existed.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'says projects may add code-review criteria through the same overlay as plan-review' with 'does not document how project code-review criteria flow through the pipeline, nor the overlay mechanism that enables them'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that ticket `fc72-bf7e-7652-4228` (adding `gate_key` to `criterion_prompt_id`) is delivered and merged before or alongside this documentation change, since the documented contract (`criterion_prompt_id(cid, gate_key="code_review")`) does not yet exist in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py`.
evidence: src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36: `def criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str) -> str:` — no gate_key parameter.
Ticket fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 status: open — the story that adds gate_key has not been delivered.
search_files for 'criterion_prompt_id.*gate_key' across src/rebar/llm returned no matches.
The plan calls this ticket the foundation child that 'lands first' and that other four seams 'consume' — its open status means the contract being documented is not yet present in the codebase.
findings.finding: The Approach section references `criterion_prompt_id(cid, gate_key="code_review") [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]` as a delivered contract the documentation section should describe, but ticket `fc72-bf7e-7652-4228` (which adds the `gate_key` keyword argument to `criterion_prompt_id`) is still `status: open`. The current implementation at `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36-42` accepts only `criterion_id` with no `gate_key` parameter, so the code being documented does not yet exist.
findings.location: Approach section — 'The `criteria eval` resolution/admission path for project code-review ids [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8]' and bullet 'resolve each to its physical prompt via `criterion_prompt_id(cid, gate_key="code_review") [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]`'
scenarios: If this documentation ticket is merged before fc72 is delivered, the documented contract (`criterion_prompt_id` with `gate_key`) will be a forward-reference to unimplemented behavior — a reader consulting the doc against the live code will find a mismatch.
The plan says this is 'the last item in the stack', implying fc72 is already landed, but fc72 is still open — the sequencing assumption is unverified.
